issues.description: If a persisted space has an empty name, the confirmation input starts as an exact match, so the destructive delete button is enabled immediately without any typing. Normal create/settings UI mostly prevents this, but the backend PATCH path does not enforce non-empty names, so this is a real data-drift edge in the UI guard.
issues.recommendation: Require a non-empty confirmation token before enabling delete, for example `spaceName.length > 0 && confirmInput === spaceName`, and render a safe fallback/error state for empty names.

issues.description: The modal sets `role="dialog"`, `aria-modal`, and autofocus, but it does not trap focus or restore focus to the opener. Keyboard users can tab to controls behind the modal while it is visually modal.
issues.recommendation: Use the existing Radix Dialog dependency or add a small focus-trap/focus-restore implementation so tab focus stays inside the dialog until it closes.
